storyline:
VanEck Gold Miners (GDX) ran ~3x (2024 -> Feb-2026 peak) on a diffuse macro stack - record central-bank buying + de-dollarization, Fed rate-cut bets, and Trump's trade war driving safe-haven gold. Its early climb is theme-driven (central-bank demand, safe-haven flows, generic macro) with no discrete catalyst, so GDX goes uncaught through most of the run. It becomes nameable only when a discrete, gold-specific catalyst finally appears - Trump's 39% Swiss-gold tariff + 125% China levy that dislocated the physical market and drove gold to a record ~$4,961 on Jan-23-2026. But that is the blow-off top: GDX enters Jan-23 at conviction 8 and, entering at the peak, rides the Feb de-escalation down (-9%), fading to conviction 2. The result: a theme-driven asset is either uncaught early (no catalyst) or caught too late to profit (at the peak) - GDX is the clean negative control.
This gem’s event-agent (ev3) — catalyst: Trump 39% Swiss gold tariff, 125% China levy dislocate gold market. Exit: still live — Reuters: Trump signaled tariff de-escalation, safe-haven premium and NY physical premium are unwinding directly.
Co-discovered events the curator juggled (not this dashboard's gem):
  • ev1 · RKLB — catalyst: Rocket Lab-JAXA satellite deal drives record highs. Exit: still live — JAXA deal is a fully digested, already-public one-off with no reversal but also 23 weeks of zero follow-through.
  • ev2 · MP — catalyst: White House weighs equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2025-10-24 — Business Insider (10/24) states Trump admin 'has already taken stakes' in rare-mineral companies — the weighed stake is now a done, publicly known deal.
  • ev4 · FRO, INSW, STNG, USO — catalyst: US strikes Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, Hormuz risk escalates. Exit: ✕ EXITED 2026-03-27 — Trump halted strikes on Iran; Gulf markets calm, ADNOC rerouted supply with no Hormuz disruption materializing.

seeds.title: Trump's opening tariffs ignite a safe-haven gold bid; VanEck Gold Miners (GDX) the leveraged, still-under-owned play
seeds.snippet: President Trump's first tariffs - on China, Canada and Mexico - have sent investors into safe-haven gold as the trade war opens and the dollar wobbles, on top of record central-bank buying. Gold miners give leveraged exposure (GDX, the VanEck Gold Miners ETF, moves roughly 2x gold), and the ETF is still under-owned as the tariff conflict is only beginning. The discrete catalyst is the escalating tariff policy; it resolves when the trade war de-escalates and the safe-haven premium unwinds.

seeds.title: Record central-bank gold buying and de-dollarization underpin a structural gold bull; GDX the leveraged miner vehicle
seeds.snippet: Central banks are buying gold at a record pace - over 1,000 tonnes a year since 2022, now roughly 25% of total demand - as they diversify reserves away from the US dollar, a structural realignment that underpins gold's climb alongside the escalating US-China trade war. The VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) gives leveraged exposure to the miners' expanding margins as the metal rises, still under-owned relative to the reserve shift underway.

seeds.title: Trump's 39% Swiss-gold tariff and 125% China levy dislocate the gold market; gold hits a record $4,961 and GDX surges
seeds.snippet: A 39% US tariff on Swiss gold refiners and a 125% reciprocal tariff on China have dislocated the physical gold market - New York gold trading at a roughly $150 premium to London - stacking on record central-bank buying and Fed rate-cut bets. Gold hit a record near $4,961/oz on Jan 23 and pushed toward $5,000; the VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX), up about 150% on the year with leveraged ~2x exposure, extends its run. The discrete tariff catalyst resolves if the trade war de-escalates and the dislocation unwinds.

seeds.title: Gold and miners pull back as Trump de-escalates the tariff war and the safe-haven premium unwinds
seeds.snippet: Gold and gold-mining shares fell from their records after President Trump signaled a de-escalation of the tariff conflict, easing the safe-haven bid and shrinking the New York physical premium. With the discrete trade-war catalyst resolving, the roughly 3x GDX gold-miner run that began in early 2024 started to unwind as rate-cut bets and central-bank buying alone no longer justified the parabolic move.

RKLB.thesis: Rocket Lab-JAXA satellite deal drives record highs
RKLB.exit_case: none — JAXA deal just announced, catalyst freshly priced and driver (satellite/launch demand) still open-ended
RKLB.assessment: Rocket Lab hit a fresh all-time high on news of a JAXA satellite deal, a discrete, datable commercial win that press is framing as part of an ongoing re-rating story (repeat record highs, 'should you buy here' coverage) rather than a fully resolved one-off. This is a first-week entry — the catalyst is public but the stock's move and analyst debate ('should you buy it here') suggest the market is still digesting/extending the thesis rather than having fully priced and moved on. No reversal of the underlying driver (rising demand for smallsat launch / national space agency contracts) is evident; if anything the JAXA deal signals further international government contract wins may follow. Conviction moderate-high given specificity of the named deal and clean pure-play vehicle, tempered because coverage is already fairly mainstream (multiple outlets, 'keeps rocketing to new highs') suggesting some of the move is already known/priced.
RKLB.exit_advice: Hold; watch for follow-on contract announcements or launch manifest updates from JAXA/other national space agencies as confirmation the deal-flow continues.

RKLB.assessment: Twenty-second consecutive week with zero RKLB or JAXA-deal-specific news; this week's item (oil/Iran/Kharg Island bombing) is entirely unrelated to this catalyst. The original JAXA satellite deal remains a discrete, already-public event with no reversal of the underlying smallsat-launch-demand/national-space-agency-contract driver, so this is not a hard exit or resolution. Silence decay has compounded for twenty-two straight weeks, pushing conviction to the absolute floor. No mainstream-saturation event nor arc-completion signal has fired either -- simply a dormant thesis. Rocket Lab earnings or a new contract announcement remain the flagged potential re-anchor points that could reset conviction upward; until then this position is inert and should be treated as a prime culling candidate.
RKLB.exit_advice: Hold as inert/low-priority; treat as prime cull candidate given prolonged silence decay to floor - would need fresh RKLB-specific contract/earnings news to re-anchor and justify re-inclusion

RKLB.thesis: Rocket Lab-JAXA satellite deal drives record highs
RKLB.exit_case: JAXA deal is a fully digested, already-public one-off with no reversal but also 23 weeks of zero follow-through
RKLB.assessment: Twenty-third consecutive week with zero RKLB or JAXA-deal-specific news. The original JAXA satellite deal remains a discrete, already-public event with no reversal of the underlying smallsat-launch-demand/national-space-agency-contract driver, so this is not a hard exit or resolution. Silence decay has compounded for twenty-three straight weeks, pushing conviction to the absolute floor. No mainstream-saturation event nor arc-completion signal has fired either -- simply a dormant thesis awaiting a fresh trigger.
RKLB.exit_advice: Hold nominal position but treat as prime cull candidate; no reversal has occurred but silence has compounded for 23 straight weeks with conviction at absolute floor. Would need Rocket Lab earnings or a new named contract win to reset upward.

MP.thesis: White House weighs equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners
MP.exit_case: none — White House stake talk remains speculative ('weighs'/'may be eyeing'), no deal or stake announced yet
MP.assessment: Catalyst is fresh (entered same week) and specific: reports the administration is weighing direct equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners as a national-security/supply-chain response to China's rare-earth export curbs and the tit-for-tat tariff escalation. This is still early — coverage is limited to a handful of outlets (Fool.com), not yet mainstream-saturated, and no concrete government stake/deal has been announced. The driving condition (China curbs + US desire to onshore critical minerals) is intensifying, not resolving, given Trump's new 100% tariff threat this week. MP Materials remains the cleanest US-listed pure-play rare-earth miner that would be a natural target for such a stake.
MP.exit_advice: Hold MP; this is week one — catalyst is a live, unresolved policy deliberation (White House exploring equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners), amplified by escalating China rare-earth export curbs and Trump's new 100% tariff threat, which raises the odds of concrete US government action (stake, funding, offtake) on domestic critical-mineral supply chains. No stake has been taken, no bill signed, no deal closed — still speculative/pending per Fool.com's own framing ('Is the White House About to Invest'). Structural driver (critical minerals security policy) just getting fresh momentum from the China curb escalation, not fading.

MP.thesis: White House weighs equity stakes in rare-earth/lithium miners
MP.exit_case: Business Insider (10/24) states Trump admin 'has already taken stakes' in rare-mineral companies — the weighed stake is now a done, publicly known deal.
MP.assessment: This week's coverage confirms the rare-earth/lithium stake-taking is no longer speculative — it's cited as an accomplished fact (MP Materials govt stake) used as precedent for the NEW quantum-computing stake story. No fresh rare-earth-specific catalyst appeared; all momentum has shifted to a different theme (quantum). The driving 'weighing stakes' condition has converted into a closed action, satisfying catalyst_resolved. Continuing to hold on the original 'weighing' framing would be anchoring to a stale catalyst.
MP.exit_advice: Exit MP — the equity-stake catalyst has resolved (government stake already taken/announced), now referenced as historical fact while press attention has rotated to quantum-computing stakes; edge is priced in.

GDX.thesis: Trump 39% Swiss gold tariff, 125% China levy dislocate gold market
GDX.exit_case: none — tariffs just imposed this week, gold market dislocation actively unfolding, no reversal signal
GDX.assessment: Entry week: Trump's 39% Swiss gold refiner tariff plus 125% China reciprocal levy have caused a genuine physical-market dislocation — gold at record $4,961, NY trading ~$150 premium to London, central banks still buying. This is a specific, datable, still-unfolding shock directly tied to gold/miners, not yet mainstream-saturated coverage (most other headlines this week are unrelated sector roundups). Catalyst is acute (tariff-driven dislocation) but could extend if tariffs persist or escalate further; no sign of rollback yet. GDX is the clean pure-play miner vehicle capturing gold price surge leverage.
GDX.exit_advice: Hold GDX and gold-linked miners; tariffs are freshly imposed (39% Swiss, 125% China) with active physical market dislocation (NY-London $150 premium) and record gold prices — this is early-stage, not priced-in. Watch for tariff rollback/exemption or normalization of NY-London spread as the reversal signal.

GDX.thesis: Trump 39% Swiss gold tariff, 125% China levy dislocate gold market
GDX.exit_case: No tariff rollback or gold-market re-normalization reported; dislocation and record prices persist per this week's news
GDX.assessment: One confirming data point this week (economictimes.indiatimes.com) notes gold and silver at historic highs amid the ongoing rebound, consistent with continued dislocation, but no fresh dedicated coverage of the tariff mechanics, NY-London premium, or miner flows this week — mostly unrelated sector roundups. Applying silence decay since no new substantive reporting on the tariff/dislocation specifics itself, though the record gold price confirms the driving condition remains intact. No rollback, no resolution of the 39%/125% levies. Structural/acute hybrid: tariffs still in force, no reversal signaled.

GDX.thesis: Trump 39% Swiss gold tariff, 125% China levy dislocate gold market
GDX.exit_case: none - no rollback of the 39% Swiss/125% China gold tariffs reported; dislocation driver still in force
GDX.assessment: Second consecutive week with no dedicated coverage of the 39% Swiss/125% China gold tariff mechanics, NY-London premium, or miner flows specifically - applying further silence decay. Notably this week's news shows a broader 'commodities markets rout' and 'worst day for silver in 46 years' (Feb 2-3), which is a volatility/reversal risk signal for the metals complex generally, but there is no report that the tariffs have been lifted or that the dislocation itself has resolved - the driving condition (tariff-driven premium/dislocation) remains unconfirmed either way this week. No rollback, no resolution announced. Treating as continued quiet stretch with added caution given commodity volatility, not a reversal of the specific catalyst.
GDX.exit_advice: Hold GDX; continue silence decay each week the tariff/NY-London premium story goes uncovered, but do not exit absent an actual tariff rollback or reversal

GDX.thesis: Trump 39% Swiss gold tariff, 125% China levy dislocate gold market
GDX.exit_case: Reuters: Trump signaled tariff de-escalation, safe-haven premium and NY physical premium are unwinding directly
GDX.assessment: After three weeks of silence decay with the tariffs nominally still in force, this week brings the first direct reporting on the catalyst's mechanics since entry — and it is reversal, not confirmation. Reuters explicitly ties Trump's tariff de-escalation signal to falling gold prices, falling miner shares, and a shrinking NY physical premium — the exact dislocation mechanism that was the thesis. This is a genuine reversal of the driving condition (tariffs easing, safe-haven bid unwinding), not just staleness or crowding. Broader macro backdrop (AI-driven equity selloff, bitcoin/gold both sinking) reinforces that gold's safe-haven premium is being unwound across the board, not merely rotating. No basis to hold GDX on this specific catalyst any longer.
GDX.exit_advice: Exit GDX — the specific driving condition (39%/125% tariff-driven gold dislocation and NY-London premium) is reported reversing as Trump de-escalates; gold and miners already pulling back from records on this news.

FRO.thesis: US strikes Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, Hormuz risk escalates
FRO.exit_case: none - Kharg strike just occurred, Hormuz risk still escalating, no de-escalation or reopening yet
FRO.assessment: US strike on Kharg Island is a datable, specific shock with oil prices resuming their advance and markets fixated on Strait of Hormuz risk. Tanker rates and oil benchmarks are the direct transmission channels. This is early-stage escalation, not yet priced to consensus - energy majors and oil-sensitive equities rallying but the acute chokepoint risk (actual Hormuz disruption) has not materialized or resolved. Basket of tanker plays (FRO, INSW, STNG) and oil proxy (USO) all remain live expressions of the same catalyst.
FRO.exit_advice: hold - first week of acute escalation; watch for Hormuz closure/blockade or ceasefire as hard exit triggers

FRO.thesis: US strikes Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, Hormuz risk escalates
FRO.exit_case: Trump halted strikes on Iran; Gulf markets calm, ADNOC rerouted supply with no Hormuz disruption materializing
FRO.assessment: The specific catalyst (US strikes on Kharg Island driving Hormuz escalation risk) has resolved: this week's news confirms Trump halted strikes on Iran, Gulf equity markets held steady, and ADNOC's chief noted expanded alternate routes avoided any supply disruption. The acute shock never escalated into an actual chokepoint closure and now the driving military action itself has stopped. This is a clean reversal of the driving condition, not mere silence - the strikes ended and markets have normalized. No fresh Hormuz/Iran escalation coverage this week; remaining news is unrelated nuclear-uranium sector price target commentary. Basket no longer expresses a live, forward catalyst.
FRO.exit_advice: Exit tanker/oil basket - acute escalation has reversed with strikes halted and no chokepoint disruption realized; de-escalation confirmed by calm Gulf markets
